threat_model: Chaos rehearsal evidence can accidentally become live fault injection if dry-run scenario evidence, staging or production target refs, runtime disruption authority, rollback execution, and terminal closure are conflated. The main risks are touching a live runtime target, mutating an event chain, retaining raw runtime logs or secrets, calling a connector during validation, bypassing UAO, and claiming resilience closure without rollback or incident handoff evidence. The mitigation is a dry-run-only schema and validator that reject live target drift, destructive authority, raw retention, missing rollback guards, and count drift.

required_checks.check: live chaos execution, staging or production targeting, runtime disruption, event-chain mutation, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success authority remain false
live runtime target refs remain denied by none:// dry-run target scope
redaction check confirms raw runtime logs and raw secret material remain denied
policy bypass check confirms Foundation Mode, dry-run-only mode, and no destructive injection remain enforced
receipt integrity check confirms scenario, evidence, rollback, UAO, LifeMeaningJudgment, EffectAssurance, SimulationReceipt, WorkerFailureReceipt, and SDLC recovery refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms scenario result and summary counts cannot drift
audit visibility check confirms the proof coverage matrix includes the chaos rehearsal execution report surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode report blocks live chaos execution, staging or production targeting, runtime disruption, event-chain mutation, connector calls, writes, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode report requires scenario and result-bank digest refs and denies raw runtime log and raw secret retention.

mitigations: keep ChaosRehearsalExecutionReport validation non-live and deterministic
require scenario refs, invariant refs, expected containment refs, required evidence refs, and rollback guard refs
require result-bank digest refs instead of raw runtime logs
deny staging and production targets in Foundation Mode
deny destructive injection, runtime disruption, event-chain mutation, connector calls, writes, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success claims
